commands.name: dotnet

options.name: --debug
options.description:
[Gray]Only to be used during [Blue]development[/] to 
include additional columns in the list for debugging purposes.[/]

options.name: --install
options.description:
[Gray]Use this option to install a new version of the 
[green]dotnet[/] Framework. If no version is defined, you will be able to 
selection from a list.[/]

options.name: --projects
options.description:
[Gray]A comma-separated list of names or partial names
to filter the projects during analysis. Ex. 
"project1,project2,project3".[/]

options.name: --runtime
options.description:
[Gray]List the available [pink1]dotnet Runtime 
Releases[/] and compare with the installed versions.[/]

options.name: --sdk
options.description:
[Gray]List the available [pink1]dotnet SDK Releases[/]
and compare with the installed versions.[/]

options.name: --silent
options.description:
[Gray]We won't ask for confirmation to continue, 
update or install. All operations will be executed.[/]

options.name: --update
options.description:
[Gray]Update the [green]dotnet[/] version of the 
project(s). If you define [blue]latest[/], the latest version will be used. If 
no version is defined, you will be able to selection from a list.[/]

arguments.name: path
arguments.description:
(Optional) [Gray]The path to the csproj or the 
solution folder it resides. If not specified, the current working directory will
be used.[/]

commands.description: Manage and evaluate Dotnet Framework versions

commands.name: npm

options.name: --debug
options.description:
[Gray]Only to be used during [Blue]development[/] to 
include additional columns in the list for debugging purposes.[/]

options.name: --ignore
options.description:
[Gray]A comma-separated list of names or partial names
to filter the packages during analysis. Ex. 
"package1,package2,package3".[/]

options.name: --install
options.description:
[Gray]Run the [DarkOrange]npm install[/] command even 
if you don't provide the [Blue]--update[/] argument. This is useful when 
checking for vulnerabilities when the package-lock.json still doesn't 
exist.[/]

options.name: --major
options.description:
[Gray]Find the latest version of the specified major 
number.[/]

options.name: --minor
options.description:
[Gray]Only evaluate the lastest version of the current
major.[/]

options.name: --packages
options.description:
[Gray]A comma-separated list of names or partial names
to filter the packages during analysis. Ex. 
"package1,package2,package3".[/]

options.name: --pre
options.description:
[Gray]Include prerelease versions when evaluating the 
latest version. (Alpha, Beta, Release Candidate, Nightly Builds, etc).[/]

options.name: --projectUrl
options.description: [Gray]Include package project url in the list.[/]

options.name: --silent
options.description:
[Gray]When using the [Blue]--update[/] argument, we 
won't ask for confirmation to continue or to install the packages. Both 
operations will be executed.[/]

options.name: --update
options.description:
[Gray]Update the packages to desired version. Latest 
version by default, lastest of the current Major when the [blue]--minor[/] 
option is specified or latest requested version when the [blue]--major 
<n>[/] option is specified.[/]

options.name: --url
options.description: [Gray]Include package URL in the list.[/]

options.name: --versionUrl
options.description: [Gray]Include package version url in the list.[/]

arguments.name: path
arguments.description:
(Optional) [Gray]The path to the package.json file or 
the folder it resides. If not specified, the current working directory will be 
used.[/]

commands.description: Manage and evaluate npm package versions

commands.name: nuget

options.name: --build
options.description:
[Gray]Run the [DarkOrange]build[/] command even if you
don't provide the [Blue]--update[/] argument. This is useful to check if 
your code is compatible with the installed packages.[/]

options.name: --debug
options.description:
[Gray]Only to be used during [Blue]development[/] to 
include additional columns in the list for debugging purposes.[/]

options.name: --ignore
options.description:
[Gray]A comma-separated list of names or partial names
to filter the packages during analysis. Ex. 
"package1,package2,package3".[/]

options.name: --install
options.description:
[Gray]Run the [DarkOrange]restore[/] command even if 
you don't provide the [Blue]--update[/] argument. This is useful when 
checking for vulnerabilities when the packages haven't being installed 
yet.[/]

options.name: --major
options.description:
[Gray]Find the latest version of the specified major 
number.[/]

options.name: --minor
options.description:
[Gray]Only evaluate the lastest version of the current
major.[/]

options.name: --packages
options.description:
[Gray]A comma-separated list of names or partial names
to filter the packages during analysis. Ex. 
"package1,package2,package3".[/]

options.name: --pre
options.description:
[Gray]Include prerelease versions when evaluating the 
latest version. (Alpha, Beta, Release Candidate, Nightly Builds, etc).[/]

options.name: --projectUrl
options.description: [Gray]Include package project url in the list.[/]

options.name: --restore
options.description:
[Gray]Run the [DarkOrange]restore[/] command before 
evaluating packages. This is useful to install the updated packages right 
away.[/]

options.name: --silent
options.description:
[Gray]When using the [Blue]--update[/] argument, we 
won't ask for confirmation to continue or to install the packages. Both 
operations will be executed.[/]

options.name: --update
options.description:
[Gray]Update the packages to desired version. Latest 
version by default, lastest of the current Major when the [blue]--minor[/] 
option is specified or latest requested version when the [blue]--major 
<n>[/] option is specified.[/]

options.name: --url
options.description: [Gray]Include package URL in the list.[/]

options.name: --versionUrl
options.description: [Gray]Include package version url in the list.[/]

arguments.name: path
arguments.description:
(Optional) [Gray]The path to the csproj or 
packages.props file or the solution folder it resides. If not specified, the 
current working directory will be used.[/]

commands.description: Manage and evaluate NuGet package versions
